Skill-eval verdicts: outage vs regression

The eval shells out to claude -p per skill. When those calls come back with no gradeable text, the harness used to score that as FAIL (0%), so an infrastructure outage was indistinguishable from a skill regression. Pick a state for each lane and see the verdict the runner now produces.

Try it

Exit 0 = measured and passed. 1 = measured and failed. 2 = could not measure (every generation died).

trigger lane
quality lane

Full matrix

triggerqualityoverallexitmeaning

The invariant that matters: a real failure in either lane always exits 1. An outage can never mask a genuine regression. That is checked in the table above: every row containing a 1 exits 1.

Why this was worth fixing

Two runs of the same branch, with an identical ci-sentinel spec and an identical description:

run A 4 dead generations -> ci-sentinel P:100 R:100 PASS run B 8 dead generations -> ci-sentinel P:0 R:0 FAIL

Nothing about the skill changed between them. The only variable was how many model calls returned an empty envelope. A gate that flips verdict on that is not measuring the skill, and a red that means "the weather was bad" trains reviewers to ignore reds that mean something.

Both lanes now distinguish no answer from a negative answer. A prompt whose every rep died is excluded from precision and recall entirely rather than counted as a miss, and dead_prompts is recorded in the results JSON so the outage stays auditable.
